reasoning: Copernicus recently reported that the first four months of 2025 were the warmest Jan-Apr on record globally. However, this is largely driven by a massive January anomaly (+0.09°C above Jan 2024). February, March, and April have all been cooler than their 2024 counterparts, with April 2025 being the 2nd warmest globally (-0.13°F / -0.07°C relative to April 2024). Given this downward trajectory relative to 2024's record warmth and the moderating effects of the recent La Niña and expected ENSO-neutral summer, 2025 will likely fall behind 2024 as the year progresses. Objective statistical models corroborate this: NOAA provides a 6.2% chance for 2025 being the warmest year on record, while Berkeley Earth provides a ~38% chance in their latest update. The community forecast of 0.65 on May 1st likely lags the increasing evidence of sub-2024 temperatures in Feb-Apr and overweights the "warmest Jan-Apr" headline. Thus, I assign a 28% probability.
